APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS ROCIO STOELO, DUC-UY PHUNG, AND PRADIP & SNEHLATA FAMILY TRUST VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 4/11/22 IS VACATED AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS 7/25/22 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY TRACKS THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE OF 4/11/22. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS THAT THIS IS THE FINAL CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT CONFORMS TO THE COURT'S RULING AND APPROVED AS TO FORM. ***order denying defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment signed after hearing.***

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment For The Purposes Of Tentative Ruling Only. Part 2 Of 2 Parts (302)
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert L. Booker, II, Esq. (888.203.1422; robert@cplglaw.com) and Doug Younglove, Esq. (doug@CPLGLaw.com) of Century Park Law Group LLP for Plaintiff via Zoom.
Frank M. La Fleur, Esq. (415.554.3826; frank.lafleur@sscityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for City and County of San Francisco.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS DUC-UY PHUNG, PRADIP & SNEHLATA FAMILY TRUST and ROCIO SOTELO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Cross-defendants Duc-Uy Phung, Rocio Sotelo, J&T Tanner Co. LLC and Pradip & Snehlata Family Trust's motion for summary judgment is denied. Section 706 of the San Francisco Public Works Code imposes a duty on the moving parties to "maintain in good repair and condition" the area where plaintiff Patricia Umanzor allegedly sustained injuries for which cross-complainant City and County of San Francico seeks indemnity from the moving parties. Section 706 also provides for a cause of action against adjacent property owners for their failure to maintain the sidewalks and sidewalk areas as well as a cause of action to the City for indemnity due the adjacent property owners failure to maintain the sidewalks and sidewalk areas. Gonzales v. City of San Jose (2004) 125 Cal. App. 4th 1127 is binding on-point authority that section 706 is not preempted by the Government Tort Claims Act. Nothing in Jordan v. City of Sacramento (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 1487 detracts from or calls into question Gonzales or that section 706 is not preempted. Because there are triable disputes whether the moving parties failed to maintain the area where Ms. Umanzor claims she was injured and whether that failure to maintain caused Ms. Umanzor's injuries (City's responses to moving parties asserted undisputed facts 2, 15 and 16), the motion is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
